# Advanced Module 

# Introduction 

In this tutorial, we will provide you with an overview of the advanced features and guide you on how to utilize them effectively. The advanced features are currently divided into two tabs: Configuration Threshold and Assigner Parameters. 

![][image1]

The Configuration Threshold section allows you to configure thresholds for various aspects, namely Free Resources, Consumers Availability, and Resource Events.

Moving on to the Assigner Parameters section, you will find additional settings to fine-tune the consumer assignment process. Here, you can set the minimum gap between consecutive special events. Furthermore, the Assigner Parameters section enables you to enable the consecutive assignment of consumers in a row.  

To access the Advanced Menu in Team2Book, please follow these steps: 

1. Start by logging into your Client Manager Team2Book account. Once you have logged in, you will be directed to the homepage of Team2Book.   
2. Look for the "Members Area" tab on the homepage and click on it.   
3. Inside the Members Area, you will find a menu.   
4. Locate and click on "Client Dashboard" in the menu.   
   

![][image2]

5. On the Client Dashboard, navigate to the bottom left corner of the screen.   
6. In that area, you will see an option labeled "Advanced." Click on it to access the Advanced Menu.

![][image3]

Let's delve into the details of each tab.

# Configuration Threshold

When using the Configuration Threshold feature in Team2Book, you have the ability to set specific time intervals or hours for various parameters. Here are the key settings you can adjust:

1. **Ignore Free Resources Less Than (hours):** By configuring this parameter, you can determine the minimum duration for which a resource needs to be available to be displayed on the "Free Resources" sub-calendar. For example, if you set it to 2 hours, any free resource available for less than 2 hours will not be shown on the sub-calendar.  
     
   ![][image4]  
   In Team2Book, when you enable the free resource and booking options in the client detail dashboard, all the available resources will be displayed on a separate sub-calendar. Consumers can then reserve resources using their availability sub-calendar. However, by utilizing the threshold configuration advanced features, you can set specific hour values. For instance, by setting the "Ignore Free Resources Less Than" parameter, resources that are free for less than the specified hour value will not be displayed on the "Free Resources" sub-calendar.   
     
2. **Ignore Consumers Availability Less Than (hours):** This setting allows you to define the minimum time interval that a consumer needs to be available for in order to be eligible for resource assignment. For instance, if you set it to 3 hours, the scheduler will not assign a resource to a consumer if their availability is less than 3 hours.  
   ![][image5]  
   Similarly, in Team2Book, the scheduler assigns a resource to a consumer if the consumer's availability aligns. Nevertheless, by configuring the "Ignore Consumers Availability Less Than" parameter, the scheduler will not assign a resource to a consumer if their availability is less than the specified hour value. By adjusting these threshold settings, you can fine-tune the resource allocation process based on your desired time intervals and ensure efficient scheduling within Team2Book.  
     
3. **Desired Minimum Gap Between Resource Events (hours):** With this configuration, you can specify the minimum time gap required between consecutive resource events. For example, if you set it to 1 hour, there must be at least a 1-hour gap between two resource events for the same resource.  Team2Book will attempt to assign consumers to its preferred resource if that resource is free for at least X hours after a previous assignment. If the number of resources is insufficient, Team2Book will ignore this parameter.  
   ![][image6]

## Constraint Tolerance For Different Time Intervals

In Team2Book, the concept of constraint refers to the minimum number of available consumers required within a specific time duration. With the Constraint Tolerance For Different Time Intervals configuration, clients now have the flexibility to modify the constraint tolerance values according to their needs. 

The Constraint Tolerance For Different Time Intervals feature allows you to set constraint tolerance levels for different time intervals, customizing them based on your specific requirements. The following parameters are available for configuration:

1. **Constraint Tolerance \- Morning (hours):** With this parameter, you can define the constraint tolerance for the morning time interval. Team2Book considers a consumer present or assigned to a resource if their availability duration for the morning falls between the start of the day \+ X hours and lunchtime \- X hours. This parameter applies only to the constrained section of the dashboard.   
     
   For example, If the value of this field is set to 1\.   
   ![][image7]  
   For example, let's assume your organization's working day is from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m., with lunchtime starting at 12:30 p.m. If the value of this field is set to 1, Team2Book will consider a consumer present in the morning if they are available from 9:30 a.m. (8:30 a.m. \+ 1 hour) to 11:30 a.m. (12:30 p.m. \- 1 hour). If the consumer is not available within this time range, they will not be counted in the number of consumers available to meet this constraint.  
     
2. **Constraint Tolerance \- Afternoon (hours):** This parameter allows you to specify the constraint tolerance for the afternoon time interval. Team2Book considers a consumer present or assigned to a resource if their availability duration for the afternoon falls between lunchtime \+ X hours and the end of the working day \- X hours. Again, this parameter applies only to the constrained section of the dashboard. 

   ![][image8]

   Using the previous example of a working day from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. with lunchtime at 12:30 p.m., if the value of this field is set to 1, Team2Book will consider a consumer present in the afternoon if they are available from 1:30 p.m. (12:30 p.m. \+ 1 hour) to 4:00 p.m. (5 p.m. \- 1 hour). If the consumer's availability does not fall within this time range, they will not be counted in the number of consumers available for compliance with this constraint.

   

3. **Constraint Tolerance \- Day (hours):** This parameter enables you to define the constraint tolerance for the entire day. Team2Book considers a consumer present or assigned to a resource if their availability duration for the day falls between the start of the day \+ X hours and the end of the working day \- X hours.   
     
   ![][image9]  
   Similar to the previous parameters, this applies only to the constrained section of the dashboard. Continuing with the example of a working day from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m., if the value of this field is set to 2, Team2Book will consider a consumer present for the day if they are available from 10:30 a.m. (8:30 a.m. \+ 2 hours) to 3:00 p.m. (5 p.m. \- 2 hours). If the consumer's availability does not fall within this time range, they will not be counted in the number of consumers available to meet this constraint.  
   

By adjusting these constraint tolerance values, you can fine-tune the scheduling process in Team2Book and ensure that consumer assignments align with your specific time intervals and availability requirements.

# Assigner Parameters

When you enable Automatic Assignment, the scheduler takes care of automatically assigning special events to the corresponding consumers.  However, if you want more control over the assignment process. In such cases, we have provided an option to manually select a consumer and customize certain advanced features. These advanced features include settings like the Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events and the Number of Consecutive Special Event Assignments.

The Assigner Parameters in the advanced features of Team2Book include two attributes: the Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events and the Number of Consecutive Special Event Assignments.

## Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events

The Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events attribute in Team2Book allows you to specify the minimum time gap required between consecutive special events assigned to a consumer. This parameter ensures that there is a specific duration of time between two consecutive special events. 

![][image10]

The "Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events" feature offers several attributes to customize the assignment process. Here are the key aspects of this feature:

* List of Consumers: This attribute allows you to view and select any consumer from a list. Once selected, the consumer's special event details will be displayed for further customization.   
* Source Special Event Type: This attribute refers to the event in which the consumer is currently participating or working.   
* Destination Special Event Type: This attribute represents the next event where the consumer will be assigned to work. The source and destination special event types can either be the same or different. For instance, if you have events named "Weekend" and "Emergency," the source and destination special event types can be "Weekend Weekend," "Emergency Emergency," "Weekend Emergency," or any other combination.   
* Minimum Gap (hrs): This attribute allows you to set the minimum time gap between consecutive special events for a consumer. By adjusting this value, you can ensure that a consumer doesn't get assigned another special event for at least the specified minimum gap duration.

To make it easier for you, the system automatically generates a list of all possible arrangements between source and destination special event types for each special event arrangement.

To provide you with a clearer understanding of the "Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events" feature, let's walk through a specific scenario. Suppose we select a consumer named Efirem Tamiru and want to customize the source and destination special event types, along with a minimum gap duration. 

![][image11]

Let's choose "Emergency AM" as both the Source Special Event Type and Destination Special Event Type. This means that Efirem Tamiru is currently participating in the "Emergency AM" event and will be assigned to the same event next. 

Next, let's set a Minimum Gap of 24 hours. This ensures that once Efirem Tamiru has completed the "Emergency AM" event, they won't be assigned another "Emergency AM" event for at least 24 hours. 

![][image12]

Click this to save the entry   
![][image13]  
You will get information for your new update  
![][image14]  
After saving these changes, go to the scheduler dashboard and click on "Assign Special Events." 

![][image15]

Select the desired Special Event Type, which in this case is "Emergency AM." Enter the corresponding date and click "Assign." 

![][image16]

By doing this, you will be able to see the newly updated event information. 

![][image17]

The scheduler calendar will reflect the change, demonstrating that Efirem Tamiru has been successfully assigned the "Emergency AM" event with the designated minimum gap.

![][image18]

Team2Book will ensure that there is at least a 1-day gap between two consecutive special events for a consumer. This means that once a consumer has participated in a special event "Emergency AM", Efirem Tamiru won't be assigned another special event for at least 24 hours. 

The Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events value can range from 0 to 744\. 

Here are a few examples:

* If you set the value to 0, it means that there is no minimum gap required between consecutive special events. This allows for immediate consecutive assignment of special events to a consumer.   
* If you set the value to 24, it indicates a minimum gap of 24 hours between consecutive special events. This ensures that a consumer has a full day of rest between two special events.   
* If you set the value to 168, it represents a minimum gap of 168 hours, equivalent to 1 week. This means that a consumer must have at least one week of rest between consecutive special events.   
* If you set the value to 744, it signifies a minimum gap of 744 hours, equivalent to 31 days. This ensures that there is a full month between consecutive special events for a consumer.

To further clarify the concept of "Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events," let's explore another example. Suppose we have a consumer named Efirem Tamiru who works the evening shift special event on Fridays but prefers not to work on the Weekend special event. 

In this case, the Source Special Event Type would be "Evening Shift" and the Destination Special Event Type would be "Weekend." Efirem Tamiru wants to ensure there is a minimum gap between these two events. 

To achieve this, we need to set a Minimum Gap of 48 hours, representing a 2-day gap (Saturday and Sunday) between the two consecutive special events. 

![][image19]

![][image20]

By saving this change in the system, we can ensure that Efirem Tamiru won't be assigned the Weekend special event for at least 48 hours after working the Evening Shift event. To observe the updated result, navigate to the scheduler dashboard and click on "Assign Special Events." Select the relevant Special Event Types, in this case, Evening Shift and Weekend. Enter the appropriate date and click "Assign." Upon doing so, you will be able to see the newly updated event information, reflecting the Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events. 

The scheduler calendar will indicate the change and ensure that Efirem Tamiru is not assigned the Weekend special event for at least 48 hours after completing the Evening Shift event. 

By adjusting the Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events parameter, you can control the frequency and spacing of special event assignments for consumers based on your specific requirements.

## Number of Consecutive Special Event Assignments

The "Number of Consecutive Events" parameter in Team2Book allows you to control the consecutive assignment of a consumer to a specific special event type. By setting this value, you can determine the number of times a consumer can be assigned the same special event type consecutively. 

With this feature, you have the ability to assign special events in a row for a consumer. For example, if you want to schedule three consecutive special events for a consumer, you can easily set the "Number of Consecutive Special Event Assignments" to 3\. This means that the consumer will be assigned three special events back-to-back without any gaps in between.

For example, if you set the value to 2, it means that a consumer (let's say Consumer \- Efirem Tamiru) can be assigned to Special Event Type \- Emergency AM twice in a row. 

![][image21]

This ensures that Efirem Tamiru participates in Special Event Type \-  Emergency AM for two consecutive occurrences. 

It's important to note that setting the value to 0 or 1 has no effect on consecutive event assignments. To enable consecutive event assignments, you need to set the value to 2 or higher. 

Additionally, there is the "Maximum Gap (hrs)" parameter, which determines the maximum time gap allowed between consecutive events. If the gap between two events exceeds the specified maximum gap, the assigner will not assign those events consecutively to a consumer. 

For example, let's say you want Consumer \- Efirem Tamiru to be assigned twice on Saturday and Sunday to a Weekend special event type. However, you don't want Consumer \- Efirem Tamiru to be assigned consecutively on Sunday and the following Saturday. In this case, you can set the Maximum Gap to 24 hours. This means that there must be at least a 24-hour gap between the Sunday and Saturday assignments for Consumer \- Efirem Tamiru.

By default, the maximum gap between consecutive special events is set to 168 hours. This means that if no specific value is assigned for the maximum gap between special events, the system will automatically assume a default gap of 168 hours, which corresponds to one week. The default setting of 168 hours ensures that there is at least a one-week gap between two consecutive special events for a consumer, unless a different value is specified. Please note that you have the flexibility to customize this setting based on your specific requirements. If you prefer a different maximum gap duration between special events, you can easily adjust the value accordingly.

In cases where both the "Minimum Gap Between Consecutive Special Events" and "Number of Consecutive Special Event Assignments" conditions are applicable to a scenario, the system will prioritize the "Number of Consecutive Special Event Assignments" condition. This means that the system will override the minimum gap requirement and schedule special events based on the specified number of consecutive assignments. 

For example, let's say a consumer has a minimum gap of 24 hours set between special events, but the "Number of Consecutive Special Event Assignments" condition is configured to schedule three consecutive special events for the same consumer. In this scenario, the system will prioritize the consecutive assignments and schedule the three events back-to-back, disregarding the minimum gap requirement. 

It's important to note that this behavior ensures that the specified number of consecutive special event assignments takes precedence over the minimum gap requirement in such cases.
